LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS PEGGY JOHNSON AND L. GIBSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF ON THE ISSUE OF WHETHER PLAINTIFF GIBSON IS DISABLED FOR PURPOSES OF THE RENT ORDINANCE. THE LETTER FROM SSA ARE INADMISIBLE HEARSAY AND GIBSON'S DECLARATION LACKS SUFFICIENT FOUNDATION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 10TH, 17TH, AND 19TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AS UNOPPOSED. GRANTED AS TO THE 14TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS CONCEDE THAT ALL THE ACTIONS IN THE COMPLAINT ARE BASED ON EVENTS THAT OCCURRED AFTER 2/8/09. DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. ORDER REGARDING DEFENDANT'S WRITTEN OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S MOTION, SIGNED AND COPIES PROVIDED TO BOTH COUNSELS. PARTIES TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER REGARDING THIS MOTION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Dec-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matt McFarland, Esq., Tenderloin Housing Clinic, 415-771-9850 for Plaintiff and moving party: Peggy Johnson
Paul A. Kanter, Esq., Law offices of Mark R. Mittelman, P.C., 925-256-0677, for Defendants and opposing party: Renella Anderson and Inez Anderson

Argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS PEGGY JOHNSON and L. GIBSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff fails to shift the burden of proof on the issue of whether plaintiff Gibson is disabled for purposes of the rent ordinance. The letter from SSA are inadmisible hearsay and Gibson's declaration lacks sufficient foundation. The motion for summary adjudication is Granted as to the 10th, 17th, and 19th affirmative defenses as unopposed. Granted as to the 14th affirmative defense because plaintiffs concede that all the actions in the complaint are based on events that occurred after 2/8/09. Denied as to all other affirmative defenses, failure to shift the burden of proof. Order regarding defendant's written objections to evidence in support of plaintiff's motion, signed and copies provided to both counsels. Parties to submit an form of order regarding this motion. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353
